banco_oraciones.oracion: Ben flew to Cartagena last winter. The ticket cost a fortune!
Kenji drank Colombian coffee a week ago and thought it was perfect.
Sofía wrote three reports last night and slept only four hours.
Abuela Carmen rode a horse to school and swam in the river.
I bought a new phone two days ago.
I didn't sleep well last night.
They didn't go to the party yesterday.
Sofía didn't drink coffee this morning.
We couldn't swim in the sea; the water was too cold.
Did you sleep well last night?
Where did Ben go last winter?
Could you swim when you were five?
Did you drink coffee this morning? Yes, I did.
Did Ben fly to Cartagena? Yes, he did.
Could Abuela Carmen ride a horse? Yes, she could.
Did they go to the party? No, they didn't.

interaccion: Ben: I **flew** to Cartagena **last winter** — the ticket **cost** a fortune!
Kenji: I **drank** Colombian coffee for the first time **a week ago**. I **thought** it was perfect.
Sofía: I **wrote** three reports **last night**... and I **slept** only four hours.
Abuela Carmen: When I was young, I **swam** in the river and **rode** a horse to school!

banner_diagnostico.opcion_a: I buyed a new phone yesterday.
banner_diagnostico.opcion_b: I bought a new phone yesterday.

inicio.definicion_pragmatica: Irregular verbs make the past without -ed — each one changes its shape, but most follow a family pattern you can learn in groups.

filas: go, do, see, take, eat, make, get, give
can → could / was able to · must, have to → had to

ejercicios.pregunta: Which verb does NOT change in the past?

ejercicios.afirmacion: 'Drink, drank, drunk' follows the i-a-u pattern, like 'sing' and 'swim'.
The past of 'can' is 'could' or 'was able to'.

ejercicios.texto: Kenji ___ (drink) Colombian coffee a week ago.
The ticket ___ (cost) a fortune last winter.

ejercicios.respuesta: Abuela Carmen rode a horse to school
Did you sleep well last night

ejercicios.texto_con_error: I buyed a new phone yesterday.
ejercicios.respuesta: I bought a new phone yesterday.

ejercicios.texto_con_error: Ben flied to Cartagena last winter.
ejercicios.respuesta: Ben flew to Cartagena last winter.
Did Kenji drink coffee this morning?

ejercicios.oracion_base: Did they go to the party?

ejercicios.respuesta: I didn't sleep well last night.

ejercicios.pregunta: Did you go to school yesterday? (short answer)
Could you swim when you were five? (short answer)

guion: Kenji: Ben, I heard you went to the beach yesterday!
Ben: Yes! I swam all morning. Then I ate fish and drank fresh juice.
Ben: I took hundreds! But then I made a classic Ben mistake...
Kenji: Oh no. What did you do?
Ben: I left my phone on the bus. I had to buy a new one.
Abuela Carmen: Don't worry, Ben. When I was young, we couldn't even take photos!

comprension.pregunta: What did Ben do at the beach in the morning?
Why did Ben have to buy a new phone?
opciones: He left his phone on the bus
comprension.respuesta: He left his phone on the bus

reading.texto: Abuela Carmen grew up in a small town near the river. Life was simple: she rode a horse to school, swam in the river every summer, and drank fresh milk from her grandfather's farm. She couldn't travel much — a trip to the city cost a lot of money — but she never felt poor. 'We made our own fun,' she says. 'I sang with my sisters every night, and my mother taught me hundreds of songs. I forgot many things in my life, but I never forgot those songs.'

preguntas.afirmacion: Abuela Carmen rode a horse to school.
She swam in the sea every summer.
A trip to the city was cheap.
Her mother taught her hundreds of songs.
